title: QUIC server-side 0-RTT receive admission closed gate
purpose: Describe the managed endpoint/runtime boundary that keeps server-side 0-RTT receive admission closed until a future anti-replay and early-data admission owner exists.
risks: If unrouted 0-RTT datagrams are treated as admission input before anti-replay exists, the server can appear to support early data without replay protection or a traced delivery contract.
If the closed gate is hidden behind the RFC discard allowance only, future work can accidentally widen server admission while still pointing at a permissive RFC requirement.
If the Retry bootstrap exception is folded into this boundary, retry-specific evidence can be confused with server 0-RTT receive support.
design_summary: The managed server receive floor keeps 0-RTT admission closed by relying on the existing endpoint route lookup boundary: Version 1 0-RTT long-header datagrams that do not match a retained live connection route are returned as unroutable and therefore do not create a connection, open streams, deliver application bytes, or install server-side ZeroRtt open material. The runtime's early-data admission observation remains false for the server role. The listener's Retry bootstrap branch remains a separate explicit exception that can consume a 0-RTT datagram only for Retry issuance; that path does not imply 0-RTT acceptance or application delivery. The closed gate is recorded as an internal CRT boundary so later receive/decrypt/admission work must add its own requirement, anti-replay design, and verification evidence before widening behavior.
alternatives_considered: Treat the existing RFC 9000 discard allowance as sufficient proof. Rejected because it proves a protocol permission but not the managed server admission boundary or public-promise guard.
Add buffering for unrouted 0-RTT datagrams. Rejected because buffering is observable admission-adjacent behavior and needs anti-replay and ownership work first.
Expose a public early-data or 0-RTT admission option while the gate is closed. Rejected because this slice intentionally preserves the current supported boundary.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Only unrouted Version 1 0-RTT long-header datagrams are owned by this closed-gate slice.
Datagrams explicitly consumed by Retry bootstrap remain under the existing Retry requirement.
The slice does not add 0-RTT decrypt, accept, buffer, stream delivery, anti-replay, transfer, retry widening, public API widening, IsSupported widening, or interop readiness.
